title: Does the cyclic group C_9215 admit a (9215,4607,2303)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would currently formalize only the verified prime-quotient slice. That would not turn this packet into a publication-ready result, because the missing ingredient is still the human mathematical closure of the residual compatibility step.
micro_paper_assessment: The selected exact case remains a valid micro-paper target if fully settled, but the audited packet only supports a structural slice on multiplier normalization and forced prime-quotient profiles. That is still on the wrong side of the title-theorem boundary for the one-shot lane.
family_affinity: high within the cyclic Hadamard residual cases with square n and three odd prime factors
generalization_signal: prime-quotient and semiprime-quotient multiplier analysis should transfer to nearby three-prime cyclic Hadamard survivors, but the final mixed-character elimination is not yet automated
strongest_honest_claim: After translation, any putative cyclic (9215,4607,2303)-difference set can be taken 2-invariant, and its prime-quotient profiles are forced to be (883,931,931,931,931) on C_5, (197,245,...,245) on C_19, and (95,47,...,47) on C_97.
paper_title_hint: Forced Prime-Quotient Profiles for Putative Cyclic (9215,4607,2303)-Difference Sets
candidate_theorem_slice: Any cyclic (9215,4607,2303)-difference set can be translated to a 2-invariant one whose prime-quotient profiles on C_5, C_19, and C_97 are uniquely forced.
next_action: Preserve the verified prime-quotient slice, cool this slug as not yet paper-ready, and only reopen it for a narrowly scoped final compatibility attack with durable candidate-local checker artifacts.
